title: pulmonaria helvetica
intro: Pulmonaria helvetica, also known as Lungwort, is a perennial plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to Europe. Its medicinal properties have been utilized for centuries, with its leaves containing compounds like pulvocarpine and pulmonarin, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Pulmonaria helvetica include its potential to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Lungwort often involve making teas or infusions from its leaves, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
